diagrams.title: Self-Referential Structure Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how a node structure is stored in memory with data and pointer components
elements: Box divided into two halves: Data section (contains actual value like int) and Pointer section (contains memory address arrow)
Arrow pointing from pointer section to another identical box representing next node
NULL symbol at the end showing termination
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that pointers store addresses, not copies, and how linked lists connect nodes dynamically

diagrams.title: Singly Linked List Visualization
diagrams.description: Linear chain of nodes with arrows showing connection direction from head to tail
elements: First box labeled HEAD with arrow to Node 1
Each node has DATA field and NEXT pointer field
Arrows connect NEXT of one node to next node's address
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how linked lists maintain order through pointers rather than contiguously like arrays

diagrams.title: Doubly Linked List Node Structure
diagrams.description: Node with three sections: previous pointer, data, and next pointer
elements: Three-section node box: LEFT arrow (prev), CENTER (data), RIGHT arrow (next)
NULL at both ends for head and tail nodes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding doubly linked list allows traversal in both directions through dual pointers

diagrams.title: Linked List Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Step-by-step showing how new node becomes head
elements: Initial list with HEAD pointing to first node
NEW node created with arrow to original first node
HEAD arrow redirected to point to NEW node
Result: NEW is now first node, followed by original list
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing pointer updates required to insert at beginning without losing reference to rest of list
